data.narrative: The student successfully applied risk-first planning by identifying multiple types of technical risk, restructuring the build order to retire uncertainty early, and adapting effectively when a business blocker invalidated part of the plan. They distinguished risk from dependency and demonstrated sound engineering judgment by introducing a stubbed payment interface rather than stopping development.

dimension_scores.observation: Recognized both technical and business constraints in the payment requirement.
dimension_scores.evidence: Identified the missing merchant account as a business blocker.

dimension_scores.observation: Maintained dependency integrity while restructuring the roadmap.
dimension_scores.evidence: Deferred payment integration while continuing unrelated features.

dimension_scores.observation: Applied multiple risk categories and reordered work to reduce uncertainty.
dimension_scores.evidence: Ranked payment integration highest and proposed a spike.

dimension_scores.observation: Defined a clear payment interface contract.
Adjusted the implementation plan quickly after the business blocker.
dimension_scores.evidence: Introduced a stubbed payment service and revised priorities.

dimension_scores.observation: Designed for future replacement of the stub with the real implementation.
dimension_scores.evidence: Separated the interface from Stripe-specific implementation.

strengths: Effectively distinguished technical risk from business risk and adapted the roadmap.
data.recommendation: Continue applying risk-first thinking while breaking large work items into smaller vertical slices.

public_evidence.student_action: Created a risk-annotated build plan and updated it with a payment stub strategy.
public_evidence.technical_detail: Risk annotations, build ordering, interface contract.
public_evidence.proof: Artifact created and referenced during Module 3.

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Prioritized payment integration and adapted to business constraints.
